title: Message Envelopes, Binary Bodies, Schemas, and Evolution · Compiler Case: Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract
summary: Consumers parse Buffer content directly into trusted domain types, overload routing keys with identity data, lack stable event IDs and versions, and break when producers add or remove fields. Planned artifact: Add and verify the asyncapi cloudevents contract increment in versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth.
Test producer and consumer compatibility for additive, optional, renamed, split, semantic, and removal changes across an overlap window.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9816 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback.
